lines.content: Hey, Caleb, it's Chris with Einstein Moving Company. How you doing?
lines.name: Chris Anderson

lines.content: Doing good man, I'm sorry about the delay, I appreciate you being patient and waiting for me.
lines.name: Chris Anderson

lines.content: Cool man. Well, let me get my notes and we'll get right after it. But this portion of the interview process pretty quick and painless.
lines.name: Chris Anderson

lines.content: Alright, so starting off some easy stuff our office is located in 78745 is that an okay commute for you?
lines.name: Chris Anderson

lines.content: Yeah, that's perfect. I'm right there on 78744 so just across the way.
Nice and then do you have a valid Texas driver's license?
lines.name: Chris Anderson

lines.content: Okay. Does it have at least two years of driving history on it? Okay. Our insurance requires or drivers to be the age of 21 or older? Do you meet that requirement?
lines.name: Chris Anderson

lines.content: Okay, and do you have reliable means of transportation to get to our office?
lines.name: Chris Anderson

lines.content: We are a smoke free va, free and drug free company. Is that gonna be an issue for you?
lines.name: Chris Anderson

lines.content: And then what do you want to join is time?
lines.name: Chris Anderson

lines.content: I'm sorry one more time I get, I apologize.
Oh, why do you want to join is time specifically?
lines.name: Chris Anderson

lines.content: Honestly, I saw that you guys you know had a, had a moving position open and I'm kind of looking for a bit of a career change you know if you've noticed our resume I was, I'm in front of plumber typically by trade but I'm just looking for something a little bit more consistent and reliable and working for you guys seem to attend the trick.
Okay, all for and how soon could you start with us if we offered you to position?
lines.name: Chris Anderson

lines.content: I'd say within the next week.
I only said up Friday. I know the onboarding process takes some time and I mean, just whenever, whenever we could get it going.
Yeah. Okay perfect, yeah, we try to keep the ball rolling as quick as possible in regards to your availability, are you looking for full or part time work?
lines.name: Chris Anderson

lines.content: Okay, we are open Monday through Sunday. Do you have open availability to be scheduled?
lines.name: Chris Anderson

lines.content: Okay, and we will honor this. Do you have any vacation time coming up?
lines.name: Chris Anderson

lines.content: There is nothing as of now, sir.
Okay, and if we brought under the team motor for strength, you can bring to the table.
lines.name: Chris Anderson

lines.content: Let's say adaptability with one of them pretty much any situation we probably I can make sure that I'm adaptable to you know, succeed in that, in that position flexibility would be another which is kind of to, to what we, what we experience the last couple of days you know, things may not always go according to plan. So you know, if something changes and I need to be flexible and, and move some things around. I can do that. I'd say leadership with another, another spring that I bring to the table I've definitely got some experience with an account manager. I've also let a team and sales operations and marketing.

So I do have some leadership under my belt and of course, I'm learning to be a better leader everyday kind of consuming different knowledge through different media outlets and, and, you know books and things like that. So leadership would be another strength. I think the last, the last thing would be communication I'm very communicative if there's anything that needs to come up anything last minute you know have forbid things like that happened but you know, I typically wanna make sure I communicate with anybody and everybody that needs to be involved so, that we can make sure that you know, we are all successful referrals.
Okay, well, Sedman all good strengths. Yeah. My last question for you on the flip side, can you give me two or three weaknesses about yourself since no one's perfect.
lines.name: Chris Anderson

lines.content: Yeah, absolutely. I think one of my one of my opportunities probably would be just being a better student learning a little bit more from other people that have more experience in their roles. It's not specifically just in moving obviously. I don't have any moving experience other than personal but in every role that I've been in sometimes I think that, hey I've got a more efficient way to do something but sometimes it's better for me to just listen right and understand that other people might have more efficiently of doing something. So, I think that would be the first thing first opportunity I will call it the second thing.

And I think the second thing is delegation, right? A lot of the time I feel like I can do things a little bit better on my own and just by taking the rains and I think that'd be better off to delegating and making sure that my team actually knows, you know, hey, this is something that I expect you to do and you know, we can kind of work.

That way I can actually you know, put my body on the line for other task and things that need to be done within the company. So yeah, I'd say that's the second one. Did you say you needed the third one?
Yeah, if you, yeah, if you can come up with a man, you're killing it so far.
lines.name: Chris Anderson

lines.content: I appreciate it. The third thing is really just organization. I, I'm getting better at it, you know, as far as staying organized making sure the calendar and everything. But, there are times where maybe I can be a little forgetful and forget to bull something on the calendar and then term that you know leads this some sort of issue on the organization side side. I am definitely getting better at it but that I'd say is an opportunity of.
Okay, well I appreciate your answer. There's man, really well put.
lines.name: Chris Anderson

lines.content: Yeah, well, yeah, I wanted to open up for before I talk about next steps, did you have any immediate questions, concerns for me.
lines.name: Chris Anderson

lines.content: The, yeah, only thing I can think about as well I guess one question. Do you guys have an on-call schedule as far as like, moving and things like that or is everything pre planned?

We put out like a two week schedule and you'll have access to that. I mean, it does change from time to time I got to run hours daily just to make sure somebody's not going too far and overtime or just kind of getting short changed on ours. So, sometimes, your assignments or start times will change kind of daily but you will know what days you're working versus off.
lines.name: Chris Anderson

lines.content: Okay, understood. Do you typically aim to, to put people on two days back to back or do you, do you like split days off?

I mean, I take in consideration what you would prefer and what your preferred days are. I tried to get those to you overtime but you know upon joining the team you're at the bottom of the total pole. So I just kinda plugging in based on scheduling needs for that time.
lines.name: Chris Anderson

lines.content: Okay, understood, but yeah, I think that's pretty much the only question I've or the other couple of questions I've got. Thank you.
Okay, well I appreciate your time and I'd love to set up an in person interview. Do you have sometime next week to come in?
lines.name: Chris Anderson

lines.content: Yeah, absolutely. What time or what availability do you have?
I mean, Wednesday is really the only problematic day for maybe we're doing our training day. So I guess, give me a date I can check my calendar.
lines.name: Chris Anderson

lines.content: We can work on Monday some sometime.
Okay, Monday, yeah, let's do in the afternoon, say like 2:00.
lines.name: Chris Anderson

lines.content: Okay, perfect. Well Caleb I'll get you over an email it'll have our address and there will be a link to click on to fill out some information in person interviews usually last about 3045 minutes stop so, I'll get that over to you and I look forward to seeing you Monday.
lines.name: Chris Anderson

lines.content: Yes, sir. I appreciate it. Oh, I did actually have one additional question and I apologize. Just based on, based on the response of kind of fitting in, you know, sitting in as of right now you saying that you need is right now as far as like filling gaps in your scheduling.

Right now, I mean, our big ticket days where we book out the most are going to be Friday, Saturday and Sunday and then also Monday as well. So, really trying to fill those gaps and depending on how many hires we make by next week. That'll kind of dictate it as well.
lines.name: Chris Anderson

lines.content: Okay, understood. With, with that said, is there, do you all ever target anything in terms of like a like a longer days for employees? Meaning like, if we're pushing that full forty or what have you, like, we tried to do ten hour days or is it most like 89 hours.

Our start time in the morning is about 7:35 sharp. I would say it's about ninety percent of the time we shoot for about a seven or nine hour workday. So ideally getting you back between 4:30 and 630 there are the days that you get way ahead of schedule and get to go home around lunchtime maybe mid afternoon on the flip side there are some seven o'clock, ten o'clock nights. We do try to avoid those, that all cost but it really just depends on the crew raising those alarms when we have a crew still out in about that we can get support out to.
lines.name: Chris Anderson

lines.content: Days understood and then one last question.

And then to double back to your, well, I wanted to double back to your first question, I guess with our scheduling needs. One other thing I wanted to say, you know, we're trying to bring on people that, you know, definitely, want to be here. Enjoy the work and ideally move up into a lead position pretty quickly because I am a little bit short on leads, I'm really trying to pinpoint people that are gonna take the training seriously and move up quickly.
lines.name: Chris Anderson

lines.content: Yes, sir. Yeah, and, and that's definitely why I ask these questions because I do wanna make sure that it is something that is kind of within, within reasonable you know, employment for myself. You know, look, looking into you guys have a moving company, I mean, what, I'm also looking for more, more physical job which is why I applied the movie companies to begin with. You know, yes, I'm looking for an apprenticeship and a plumber, but I'm also looking for something that's gonna keep me active throughout the, throughout the you know, throughout my employment. I should say. Yeah.

Yeah, well you'll stay, you'll definitely stay active man. That's what kinda drew me to the business is you're getting paid to stay in really good shape. So it was a win-win for me. I was pretty young whenever I got an industry, I was about 24 and it was perfect for my needs. I was not married, no kids at that point. So yeah, had a lot of freedom and it was a lot of fun at the end of the day.
lines.name: Chris Anderson

lines.content: Awesome. Well that's good to, good to hear the last question I did have with that lead in training. So what if you could refresh my memory the salary range. What was the salary range in the training?

So with the lead and training role, you make $20 an hour for the base pay, we expect at least $7 an hour and tips based on our credit card transactions that is not account for cash tip or Venmo tips. I usually see closer to about $10 an hour, based on my cruise feedback and what they're telling me training can last anywhere from about a month and a half to six months. Really depending on the individual and house here. So the serious are taking the training and then once you move up into a part time lead role any day that you're leading you're making $21 an hour, plus you get the chance at bonuses once the quarter is ended.
lines.name: Chris Anderson

lines.content: Okay, ome. Alright, yeah, all that sounds great and you said Monday at about 2:00?
Yeah, Monday at two and I'll get you over that email with the address and then that link for you to click on and fill out that information before coming over.
lines.name: Chris Anderson

lines.content: Yeah, you're very welcome. Take care.
lines.name: Chris Anderson
